---
name: Suggest a concept
about: Propose a new core concept for the curriculum (added by discussion first, then code)
title: "[Concept] <short name>"
labels: ["concept-suggestion"]
---

<!--
New CORE concepts are added by issue FIRST, so we can align on whether
they belong before any code is written. The bar is high on purpose: the
25 core concepts are a textbook, not a feed. New-but-foundational ideas
belong here; new-and-newsy ideas usually belong in a what's-new card
instead (use the other template).
-->

## Concept
What is the concept, in one sentence?

## Why it belongs in the CORE curriculum
Why is this foundational for an AI PM (not just timely)? Will it still
matter in five years, like tokenisation or evals?

## Which chapter, and where in the sequence?
- [ ] Ch 1 — ML Foundations
- [ ] Ch 2 — How LLMs Work
- [ ] Ch 3 — Evals, Safety & Cost

## The four-part pattern (rough draft is fine)
1. **Core idea** (one sentence, key phrase bolded):
2. **Plain-English explanation** (2–3 paragraphs, no math):
3. **Interactive widget idea** (what does the reader drag/click/step?):
4. **PM takeaway** (when to use it, what question to ask, what red flag):
5. **Interview line** (one quotable sentence):

## Could this be a what's-new card instead?
If it's a recent development rather than a foundation, say so — it may fit
the supplementary layer better.
